import json

Be prepare to have hypertension after it lol.", "date": "2018-01-02 21:35:31"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1500", "user_id": "userid_1030", "business_ref": "businessref_37", "rating": "1", "useful": "3", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Kelly is hands down the best orange theory trainer I have ever encountered. She is also the only reason I put up with the atrocious customer service at this location and unfair charges for so long. I have been having issues with the app not booking me in classes, showing me as not in classes I am booked in, and not cancelling classes I cancelled in time. This has resulted in several disputable charges on my account. Each time I called I was told there were no other reported issues with the app and I must be mistaken. And that if there was an issue with the app, they are just a franchise and the app is owned and ran by orange theory not the franchise location. This went on for months and to be perfectly honest I thought maybe I was mistaken. Last week I was booked in Kelly's 10 am Saturday class and I screenshotted it in the app as booked and screenshotted it in my calendar (as this is also done via the app). I was on the wait list and often check my wait list number throughout the week. On Friday when I went to see if I had been bumped up, I was no longer booked in the class. When I called to ask them about the app, she told me I was still on the wait list as number 2. At this point I flipped out because had I not called I would have just found another workout for that morning assuming I was again mistaken AGAIN and thus "no showed" thinking I wasn't booked in the class when I was. Thus, resulting in a charge for a class I "no showed", when in reality the app is not providing accurate info but I am making decisions as though it is. Thank god I screenshotted it or else they could have continued dodging me and saying I am mistaken. I asked Emily if I could please have a number to speak with someone at orange theory about the app issues. After being on hold for 10 minutes she came back and said that the manager would call me. It has been almost a week and no call. I am cancelling my membership today, but if you want good customer service, accountability, and you don't want to be robbed blind, I suggest you use a different orange theory location. I am genuinely upset about this because like I said, Kelly is worth the bullshit because she is such an amazing trainer. They are lucky to have her and most people probably put up with their bullshit because of her. I will probably pay the higher "drop in fee" to get my Kelly fix, but I won't let them have access to any of my cards via a membership. They will take your money with zero remorse as to whether you or their app caused the issue. They don't care.", "date": "28 Sep 2016, 17:19"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1481", "user_id": "userid_597", "business_ref": "businessref_44", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Sat for 15 minutes no service, not even waters. The store carries many unique products that I cannot find in generic grocery stores.  The problem is the service.  On a Saturday (prime shopping day for most), they don't seem to staff the deli and meat counter with enough people.  In the deli you can wait 5 to 10 minutes watching staff making food and filling the food cases but they do not have the responsibility to wait on customers. args.code:
import json

# Get the reviews data file
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22

# Read the data
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

print(f"Total reviews since 2016 by 2016 users: {len(reviews)}")
print(f"Sample review:", reviews[0])

# Count reviews per user to see distribution
from collections import Counter
user_review_counts = Counter([r['user_id'] for r in reviews])
print(f"Users with reviews: {len(user_review_counts)}")
print(f"Top 5 users by review count:", user_review_counts.most_common(5))

result = json.dumps({
    'total_reviews': len(reviews),
    'unique_users_with_reviews': len(user_review_counts),
    'sample_user_review_counts': user_review_counts.most_common(5)
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Get reviews data
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = reviews_data

# Extract business references
business_refs = list(set([r['business_ref'] for r in reviews]))
print(f'Unique businesses reviewed: {len(business_refs)}')
print(f'Sample business refs: {business_refs[:10]}')

# Create mapping of review counts per business
business_review_counts = Counter([r['business_ref'] for r in reviews])

result = json.dumps({
    'unique_businesses': len(business_refs),
    'sample_business_refs': business_refs[:5],
    'business_review_counts_sample': dict(list(business_review_counts.items())[:5])
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
import re

# Get reviews data
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = reviews_data

# Get all unique business references that were reviewed
all_business_refs = list(set([r['business_ref'] for r in reviews]))
print(f'Total unique businesses reviewed: {len(all_business_refs)}')

# Convert business_ref to business_id format (remove 'businessref_' and add 'businessid_')
business_ids = [ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_') for ref in all_business_refs]
print(f'Converted to {len(business_ids)} business IDs')
print(f'First 10: {business_ids[:10]}')

# Now I need to query all these businesses from MongoDB
result = json.dumps({
    'total_business_ids': len(business_ids),
    'business_ids_sample': business_ids[:5]
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Get reviews data
reviews_path = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Get users who registered in 2016
users_path = var_functions.query_db:20
users_2016 = locals().get(users_path, [])
if isinstance(users_2016, list):
    user_ids_2016 = [u['user_id'] for u in users_2016]
else:
    with open(users_2016, 'r') as f:
        users = json.load(f)
    user_ids_2016 = [u['user_id'] for u in users]

print(f"Number of 2016 users: {len(user_ids_2016)}")
print(f"Total reviews: {len(reviews)}")

# Get all business data
businesses_path = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f"Total businesses in database: {len(businesses)}")

# Create mapping from business_id to description (which contains categories)
business_desc_map = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Extract categories from descriptions
def extract_categories(description):
    """Extract categories from description text"""
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Pattern: "services in Education, Elementary Schools, ..." or "specializes in Vietnamese, Soup, ..." or "offers ... in the categories of ..."
    patterns = [
        r'services in ([^\.]+)',
        r'specializes in ([^\.]+)',
        r'categories of ([^\.]+)',
        r'range of services including ([^\.]+)',
        r'offers a range of services in ([^\.]+)',
        r'offers a (?:wide range|delightful array|diverse selection|tempting selection|variety) of ([^\.]+)',
        r'provides essential services in the categories of ([^\.]+)',
        r'making it an ideal choice for ([^\.]+)',
        r'specializes in ([^,]+(?:, [^,]+)+)',
        r'offers ([^,]+(?:, [^,]+)+)(?:, making it|, perfect for|, ideal for)'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and clean up
            categories = [cat.strip() for cat in text.split(',')]
            # Clean up any "and" at the end
            categories = [re.sub(r'\sand$', '', cat).strip() for cat in categories]
            return [cat for cat in categories if cat]
    
    # If no pattern matched, try to find list in the description
    if ',' in description:
        # Find the last part that might be the categories
        parts = description.split(',')
        # Look for the part that has "services", "specializes", "categories"
        for i, part in enumerate(parts):
            if any(word in part.lower() for word in ['service', 'specializ', 'category', 'offers', 'including']):
                # Return from this part onwards
                cats = parts[i:]
                # Clean up the first one
                cats[0] = re.sub(r'.*?(services in|specializes in|categories of|including|offers)', '', cats[0], flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                return [cat.strip() for cat in cats if cat.strip()]
    
    return []

# Count categories across all reviewed businesses
category_counts = Counter()
business_category_map = {}

# Also track review counts per business_id
business_review_counts = Counter()
for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref', '')
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f"Total unique businesses reviewed by 2016 users: {len(business_review_counts)}")

# Create category counts weighted by review count
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    description = business_desc_map.get(business_id, '')
    categories = extract_categories(description)
    
    if categories:
        business_category_map[business_id] = categories
        for category in categories:
            category_counts[category] += review_count
    else:
        # Try to find some categories in the description even if pattern didn't match
        if description:
            # Simple approach: look for capitalized words or known category patterns
            words = description.split()
            potential_categories = []
            for word in words:
                if word[0].isupper() and len(word) > 3:
                    potential_categories.append(word)
            if potential_categories:
                category_counts['Uncategorized'] += review_count

print(f"Total categories found: {len(category_counts)}")
print(f"Top categories: {category_counts.most_common(10)}")

# Get top 5 categories
top_5_categories = category_counts.most_common(5)

result = json.dumps({
    'top_5_categories': top_5_categories,
    'total_businesses_reviewed': len(business_review_counts),
    'total_category_counts': len(category_counts)
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

    businesses = json.load(f)

# Create mapping from business_id to description
business_id_to_desc = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Count reviews per business (using business_id format)
business_review_counts = Counter()
for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f'Total reviews: {len(reviews)}')
print(f'Unique businesses: {len(business_review_counts)}')

# Extract categories from descriptions
# Look for patterns like "services in A, B, and C" or "including A, B, C"
category_review_counts = Counter()
business_categories = {}

for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    description = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    categories = []
    
    if description:
        # Method 1: Look for "services in" pattern
        match = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.\-]+)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            text = match.group(1)
            # Split by comma and clean
            cats = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',')]
            categories = [c for c in cats if c and len(c) > 2]
        
        # Method 2: Look for "including" pattern
        if not categories:
            match = re.search(r'including ([^\.\-\,]+)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
            if match:
                text = match.group(1)
                cats = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',')]
                categories = [c for c in cats if c and len(c) > 2]
        
        # Method 3: Look for categories at end of description
        if not categories:
            # Find the part after "services", "specializes", or "categories"
            parts = description.split(',')
            # Get the last 3-4 parts which are likely categories
            if len(parts) > 1:
                categories = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip() and len(p.strip()) > 2]
        
        # Debug: print unhandled descriptions
        if not categories and 'services' in description.lower():
            print(f'Unhandled: {description[:100]}')
    
    business_categories[business_id] = categories
    
    # Add to category counts weighted by review count
    if categories:
        for category in categories:
            category_review_counts[category] += review_count

print(f'Total categories: {len(category_review_counts)}')
print(f'Top 5 categories: {category_review_counts.most_common(5)}')

result = json.dumps({
    'top_5_categories': category_review_counts.most_common(5),
    'unique_businesses': len(business_review_counts),
    'unique_categories': len(category_review_counts)
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load data
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Create business_id -> description map
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Function to extract categories
def extract_cats(desc):
    if not desc:
        return []
    # Find text between 'services in' and period or end
    match = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    # Find text between 'including' and comma or period
    match = re.search(r'including ([^\.\,]+)', desc, re.I)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    # Last resort: split description by comma and take last parts
    parts = desc.split(',')
    if len(parts) > 1:
        # Take last 3 non-empty parts
        cats = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
        # Filter short words
        return [c for c in cats if len(c) > 3]
    return []

# Count review counts per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[bid] += 1

print(f'Found {len(business_review_counts)} unique businesses reviewed')

# Calculate category review totals
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    cats = extract_cats(desc)
    if cats:
        for cat in cats:
            category_totals[cat] += review_count

# Show top categories
print('Top 10 categories:')
for cat, count in category_totals.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

top_5 = category_totals.most_common(5)
result = json.dumps({'top_5': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Load data
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Create business_id -> description map
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Function to extract categories
def extract_cats(desc):
    if not desc:
        return []
    # Find text between 'services in' and period or end
    match = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    # Find text between 'including' and comma or period
    match = re.search(r'including ([^\.\,]+)', desc, re.I)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    # Last resort: split description by comma and take last parts
    parts = desc.split(',')
    if len(parts) > 1:
        # Take last 3 non-empty parts
        cats = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
        # Filter short words
        return [c for c in cats if len(c) > 3]
    return []

# Count review counts per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[bid] += 1

print(f'Found {len(business_review_counts)} unique businesses reviewed')

# Calculate category review totals
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    cats = extract_cats(desc)
    if cats:
        for cat in cats:
            category_totals[cat] += review_count

# Show top categories
print('Top 10 categories:')
for cat, count in category_totals.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

top_5 = category_totals.most_common(5)
result = json.dumps({'top_5': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews
review_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(review_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses
business_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Mapping
business_desc = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Count reviews per business
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[bid] += 1

print(f'Businesses reviewed: {len(review_counts)}')

# Get categories
cat_counts = Counter()
for bid, count in review_counts.items():
    desc = business_desc.get(bid, '')
    if desc:
        # Extract after 'services in' or last items
        m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        if m:
            cats = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
        else:
            # Last few items
            parts = desc.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
        
        for cat in cats:
            if len(cat) > 3:
                cat_counts[cat] += count

print('Top 10:')
for cat, count in cat_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'{cat}: {count}')

# Top 5
result = json.dumps({'top_5': cat_counts.most_common(5)})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews
review_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(review_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses
business_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Mapping
business_desc = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Count reviews per business
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[bid] += 1

print(f'Businesses reviewed: {len(review_counts)}')

# Get categories
cat_counts = Counter()
for bid, count in review_counts.items():
    desc = business_desc.get(bid, '')
    if desc:
        # Extract after 'services in' or last items
        m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        if m:
            cats = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
        else:
            # Last few items
            parts = desc.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
        
        for cat in cats:
            if len(cat) > 3:
                cat_counts[cat] += count

print('Top 10:')
for cat, count in cat_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'{cat}: {count}')

# Top 5
result = json.dumps({'top_5': cat_counts.most_common(5)})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews - get the file path
reviews_info = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
if isinstance(reviews_info, str):
    with open(reviews_info, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = reviews_info

# Load businesses - get the file path  
businesses_info = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']
if isinstance(businesses_info, str):
    with open(businesses_info, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = businesses_info

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Create business_id -> description map
business_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business, converting refs to ids
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    ref = r.get('business_ref', '')
    bid = ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[bid] += 1

print(f'{len(review_counts)} businesses were reviewed')

# Extract categories from descriptions and weight by review count
category_counts = Counter()
for bid, rcount in review_counts.items():
    desc = business_desc.get(bid, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    # Pattern matching for categories
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
    if m:
        cats = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    else:
        # Fallback to last parts of description
        parts = desc.split(',')
        cats = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
    
    for cat in cats:
        if len(cat) > 3:  # Skip very short items
            category_counts[cat] += rcount

print('Top 10 categories with review counts:')
for cat, cnt in category_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {cnt}')

result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': category_counts.most_common(5)})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

            category_counts[cat] += rcount

print('Top 10 categories with review counts:')
for cat, cnt in category_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {cnt}')

result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': category_counts.most_common(5)})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load data
reviews_path = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

businesses_path = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Processing {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Map business_id to description
business_id_to_desc = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Count review counts per business (converting business_ref to business_id)
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r.get('business_ref', '')
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f'Found reviews for {len(business_review_counts)} unique businesses')

# Extract categories from descriptions
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Pattern: "services in Education, Elementary Schools, Child Care & Day Care, Local Services, Preschools, and Montessori Schools."
    match = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        # Split by comma and clean up
        categories = []
        for cat in text.split(','):
            cat = cat.strip()
            # Remove "and" from the beginning if present
            cat = re.sub(r'^and\s+', '', cat)
            if cat:
                categories.append(cat)
        return categories
    
    # Alternative pattern: "including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, Hair Stylists" 
    match = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in text.split(',') if cat.strip()]
        return categories
    
    # Alternative pattern: "offers a range of services including A, B, C"
    match = re.search(r'services including ([^\.]+)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in text.split(',') if cat.strip()]
        return categories
    
    # Alternative pattern: "in the categories of A, B, C"
    match = re.search(r'categories of ([^\.]+)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in text.split(',') if cat.strip()]
        return categories
    
    # Alternative: "specializes in Vietnamese, Soup, Restaurants"
    match = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        # Usually ends with "offering" or similar
        text = re.split(r'\boffering\b|\bmaking\b|\bto\b|\band\b', text)[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in text.split(',') if cat.strip()]
        return categories
    
    # If none of the above, try to extract from end of description
    # Usually descriptions list categories at the end
    parts = description.split(',')
    if len(parts) >= 3:
        # Take the last 3-5 parts which are likely categories
        potential_categories = parts[-5:]
        categories = []
        for cat in potential_categories:
            cat = cat.strip()
            # Remove common prefixes
            cat = re.sub(r'^(providing|offering|and|or)\s+', '', cat, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
            # Remove location prefix if present
            cat = re.sub(r'^Located at [^,]+,\s*', '', cat)
            if cat and len(cat) > 3:
                categories.append(cat)
        if categories:
            return categories
    
    return []

# Count total reviews per category
category_review_totals = Counter()
business_categories_map = {}
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    description = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    categories = extract_categories(description)
    business_categories_map[business_id] = categories
    
    for category in categories:
        category_review_totals[category] += review_count

# Show some examples
print('\nSample business categories:')
sample_ids = list(business_review_counts.items())[:5]
for bid, count in sample_ids:
    cats = business_categories_map.get(bid, [])
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(bid, '')[:100]
    print(f'  {bid}: {count} reviews -> {cats}')
    print(f'    Desc: {desc}...')

print(f'\nTotal unique categories found: {len(category_review_totals)}')
print(f'Top 15 categories by review count:')
for cat, count in category_review_totals.most_common(15):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

# Get top 5
top_5 = category_review_totals.most_common(5)
result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    description = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    categories = extract_categories(description)
    business_categories_map[business_id] = categories
    
    for category in categories:
        category_review_totals[category] += review_count

# Show some examples
print('\nSample business categories:')
sample_ids = list(business_review_counts.items())[:5]
for bid, count in sample_ids:
    cats = business_categories_map.get(bid, [])
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(bid, '')[:100]
    print(f'  {bid}: {count} reviews -> {cats}')
    print(f'    Desc: {desc}...')

print(f'\nTotal unique categories found: {len(category_review_totals)}')
print(f'Top 15 categories by review count:')
for cat, count in category_review_totals.most_common(15):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

# Get top 5
top_5 = category_review_totals.most_common(5)
result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses
businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Create business_id -> description map
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business (convert business_ref to business_id)
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f'Found {len(business_review_counts)} unique businesses reviewed')

# Extract categories from descriptions
def get_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Pattern 1: services in X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 2: including X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 3: specializes in X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        # Remove trailing description
        text = re.split(r'\boffering\b|\bmaking\b|\bto\b', text)[0]
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 4: destination for X, Y
    m = re.search(r'destination for ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 5: fields of X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Fallback: last 3-4 comma-separated items
    parts = description.split(',')
    if len(parts) >= 3:
        candidates = parts[-4:]
        cats = []
        for c in candidates:
            c = c.strip()
            c = re.sub(r'^(providing|offering|and|or|with)\s+', '', c, flags=re.I)
            if c and len(c) > 3:
                cats.append(c)
        return cats
    
    return []

# Count category review totals
category_totals = Counter()
sample_data = []

for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    categories = get_categories(desc)
    
    if len(sample_data) < 5:
        sample_data.append({
            'business_id': business_id,
            'review_count': review_count,
            'categories': categories,
            'desc_preview': desc[:80]
        })
    
    for cat in categories:
        # Clean category further
        cat = re.sub(r'\.$', '', cat)  # Remove trailing period
        if cat and len(cat) > 2:
            category_totals[cat] += review_count

print(f'Found {len(category_totals)} unique categories')
print(f'Top 15:')
for cat, count in category_totals.most_common(15):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

print('\nSample data:')
for s in sample_data:
    print(f"  {s['business_id']}: {s['review_count']} reviews")
    print(f"    Categories: {s['categories']}")

result = json.dumps({
    'top_5_categories': category_totals.most_common(5),
    'total_category_count': len(category_totals),
    'total_businesses': len(business_review_counts)
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

        cat = re.sub(r'\.$', '', cat)  # Remove trailing period
        if cat and len(cat) > 2:
            category_totals[cat] += review_count

print(f'Found {len(category_totals)} unique categories')
print(f'Top 15:')
for cat, count in category_totals.most_common(15):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

print('\nSample data:')
for s in sample_data:
    print(f"  {s['business_id']}: {s['review_count']} reviews")
    print(f"    Categories: {s['categories']}")

result = json.dumps({
    'top_5_categories': category_totals.most_common(5),
    'total_category_count': len(category_totals),
    'total_businesses': len(business_review_counts)
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses
businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Create business_id -> description map
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f'Found {len(business_review_counts)} unique businesses reviewed')

# Extract categories from descriptions
def get_categories(description):
    if not description:
        return []
    
    # Pattern 1: services in X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 2: including X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 3: specializes in X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        # Remove trailing verbs
        text = text.split(' making ')[0].split(' offering ')[0].split(' to ')[0]
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 4: destination for X, Y
    m = re.search(r'destination for ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 5: fields of X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', description, re.I)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        return [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    
    # Fallback: last comma-separated items
    parts = description.split(',')
    if len(parts) >= 3:
        cats = []
        last_parts = parts[-4:]
        for part in last_parts:
            part = part.strip()
            part = re.sub(r'^(providing|offering|and|or|with)\s+', '', part, flags=re.I)
            part = re.sub(r'^(located at|this|facility|establishment|business)\s+.*$', '', part, flags=re.I)
            if part and len(part) > 3:
                cats.append(part)
        return cats
    
    return []

# Count category totals
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    categories = get_categories(desc)
    
    for category in categories:
        # Clean category further
        category = re.sub(r'\.$', '', category)
        category = category.strip()
        if category and len(category) > 2:
            category_totals[category] += review_count

print(f'Found {len(category_totals)} unique categories')
print('Top 10:')
for cat, count in category_totals.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

# Top 5
top_5 = category_totals.most_common(5)
result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses  
businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print('Loaded', len(reviews), 'reviews and', len(businesses), 'businesses')

# Map business_id to description
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

# Extract categories and count
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Try different patterns
    categories = []
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
    if m:
        categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    else:
        m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        if m:
            categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
        else:
            m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
            if m:
                text = m.group(1)
                categories = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
            else:
                parts = desc.split(',')
                if len(parts) > 1:
                    categories = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
    
    for category in categories:
        category = re.sub(r'\.$', '', category).strip()
        if len(category) > 3:
            category_totals[category] += review_count

print('Top 10 categories:', category_totals.most_common(10))
result = json.dumps({'top_5': category_totals.most_common(5)})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

val_args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses  
businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print('Loaded', len(reviews), 'reviews and', len(businesses), 'businesses')

# Map business_id to description
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

# Extract categories and count
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Try different patterns
    categories = []
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
    if m:
        categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    else:
        m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        if m:
            categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
        else:
            m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
            if m:
                text = m.group(1)
                categories = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
            else:
                parts = desc.split(',')
                if len(parts) > 1:
                    categories = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
    
    for category in categories:
        category = re.sub(r'\.$', '', category).strip()
        if len(category) > 3:
            category_totals[category] += review_count

print('Top 10 categories:', category_totals.most_common(10))
result = json.dumps({'top_5': category_totals.most_common(5)})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load data
reviews_path = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

businesses_path = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Create mapping
business_id_to_desc = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

# Extract categories and count total reviews per category
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Find category patterns
    categories = []
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
    if m:
        categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    else:
        m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        if m:
            categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
        else:
            m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
            if m:
                text = m.group(1)
                categories = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
            else:
                # Fallback - get last parts
                parts = desc.split(',')
                if len(parts) > 2:
                    categories = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
    
    # Clean and count
    for category in categories:
        category = re.sub(r'\.$', '', category).strip()
        if len(category) > 3:
            category_totals[category] += review_count

# Top 5
top_5 = category_totals.most_common(5)
output = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(output)

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews and businesses
reviews_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
businesses_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

with open(reviews_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

with open(businesses_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Map business_id to description
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

# Count category totals
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Pattern 1: services in X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
    if m:
        categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    # Pattern 2: including X, Y, Z
    elif re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I):
        m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    # Pattern 3: specializes in X, Y, Z
    elif re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I):
        m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        text = m.group(1)
        categories = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
    # Fallback
    else:
        parts = desc.split(',')
        categories = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
    
    for category in categories:
        category = re.sub(r'\.$', '', category).strip()
        if category and len(category) > 3:
            category_totals[category] += review_count

print('Top 10:', category_totals.most_common(10))
result = json.dumps({'top_5': category_totals.most_common(5)})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews (it's a file path)
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses (it's already in memory as a list)
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Create business_id -> description map
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f'Processing {len(business_review_counts)} businesses with reviews')

# Extract categories and count total reviews per category
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Try multiple patterns to extract categories
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern: services in X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
    if m:
        categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    else:
        # Pattern: including X, Y, Z
        m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        if m:
            categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
        else:
            # Pattern: specializes in X, Y, Z
            m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
            if m:
                text = m.group(1)
                categories = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
            else:
                # Fallback: take last few comma-separated items
                parts = desc.split(',')
                if len(parts) >= 3:
                    categories = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
    
    # Clean and count categories
    for category in categories:
        category = category.strip()
        category = re.sub(r'\.$', '', category)  # Remove trailing period
        
        # Skip very short or generic terms
        if len(category) <= 2 or category.lower() in ['and', 'or', 'the', 'this', 'that']:
            continue
            
        category_totals[category] += review_count

print('Top 10 categories by total reviews:')
for cat, count in category_totals.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

# Get top 5
top_5_categories = category_totals.most_common(5)

result = json.dumps({
    'top_5_categories': top_5_categories,
    'total_categories': len(category_totals),
    'total_businesses_reviewed': len(business_review_counts)
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews (it's a file path)
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:22
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses (it's already in memory as a list)
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Create business_id -> description map
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_ref = r['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f'Processing {len(business_review_counts)} businesses with reviews')

# Extract categories and count total reviews per category
category_totals = Counter()
for business_id, review_count in business_review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Try multiple patterns to extract categories
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern: services in X, Y, Z
    m = re.search(r'services? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
    if m:
        categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
    else:
        # Pattern: including X, Y, Z
        m = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
        if m:
            categories = [c.strip() for c in m.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
        else:
            # Pattern: specializes in X, Y, Z
            m = re.search(r'specializes? in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.I)
            if m:
                text = m.group(1)
                categories = [c.strip() for c in text.split(',') if c.strip()]
            else:
                # Fallback: take last few comma-separated items
                parts = desc.split(',')
                if len(parts) >= 3:
                    categories = [p.strip() for p in parts[-3:] if p.strip()]
    
    # Clean and count categories
    for category in categories:
        category = category.strip()
        category = re.sub(r'\.$', '', category)  # Remove trailing period
        
        # Skip very short or generic terms
        if len(category) <= 2 or category.lower() in ['and', 'or', 'the', 'this', 'that']:
            continue
            
        category_totals[category] += review_count

print('Top 10 categories by total reviews:')
for cat, count in category_totals.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {count}')

# Get top 5
top_5_categories = category_totals.most_common(5)

result = json.dumps({
    'top_5_categories': top_5_categories,
    'total_categories': len(category_totals),
    'total_businesses_reviewed': len(business_review_counts)
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews from file
reviews_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses data (already loaded from MongoDB query)
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

print(f'Loaded {len(reviews)} reviews and {len(businesses)} businesses')

# Create business_id description mapping
business_id_desc = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Count reviews per business
business_review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_id = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    business_review_counts[business_id] += 1

print(f'Found reviews for {len(business_review_counts)} businesses')

# Extract categories efficiently
# Most of these businesses are in the beauty, food, and service industries
# Let's do a simpler extraction to avoid the regex issues

# Just examine the descriptions manually and categorize
# For each business, look at its description and pull out the clearest categories

category_review_counts = Counter()

# Track a few examples
debug_info = []

for business_id, review_count in list(business_review_counts.items())[:120]:  # Process all but in batches to avoid timeout
    desc = business_id_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Extract categories - simple approach: split by comma and take meaningful parts
    parts = desc.split(',')
    
    categories = []
    for part in parts:
        part = part.strip()
        # Remove location info
        if 'Located at' in part or 'this facility' in part or 'this establishment' in part:
            continue
        # Clean up
        part = part.replace('providing a range of services in', '').replace('offering a wide range of services, including', '').strip()
        part = part.replace('offering', '').replace('specializes in', '').strip()
        
        if part and len(part) > 3:
            # Take the first few words as category
            words = part.split()[:4]
            category = ' '.join(words)
            # Clean up common endings
            category = category.split('.')[0].split(' and ')[0].strip()
            if category:
                categories.append(category)
    
    # Limit to unique categories
    categories = list(set(categories))
    
    # Count reviews for each category
    for category in categories[:3]:  # Limit to first 3 to avoid overcounting
        # Skip generic terms
        if category.lower() in ['this', 'that', 'and', 'or', 'the']:
            continue
        category_review_counts[category] += review_count
    
    if len(debug_info) < 3:
        debug_info.append({
            'business_id': business_id,
            'review_count': review_count,
            'categories': categories,
            'desc_preview': desc[:80]
        })

print(f'Processed sample, found {len(category_review_counts)} categories')
print('Top categories so far:')
for cat, cnt in category_review_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {cnt}')

# Need to process all - let me do this in a smarter way
result = json.dumps({
    'categories_sample': category_review_counts.most_common(15),
    'debug': debug_info
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

            # Take the first few words as category
            words = part.split()[:4]
            category = ' '.join(words)
            # Clean up common endings
            category = category.split('.')[0].split(' and ')[0].strip()
            if category:
                categories.append(category)
    
    # Limit to unique categories
    categories = list(set(categories))
    
    # Count reviews for each category
    for category in categories[:3]:  # Limit to first 3 to avoid overcounting
        # Skip generic terms
        if category.lower() in ['this', 'that', 'and', 'or', 'the']:
            continue
        category_review_counts[category] += review_count
    
    if len(debug_info) < 3:
        debug_info.append({
            'business_id': business_id,
            'review_count': review_count,
            'categories': categories,
            'desc_preview': desc[:80]
        })

print(f'Processed sample, found {len(category_review_counts)} categories')
print('Top categories so far:')
for cat, cnt in category_review_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {cnt}')

# Need to process all - let me do this in a smarter way
result = json.dumps({
    'categories_sample': category_review_counts.most_common(15),
    'debug': debug_info
})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews data
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = reviews_data

# Load businesses data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:68']

# Create mapping
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[bid] += 1

# Extract categories
# Most descriptions follow patterns like:
# "services in A, B, C, and D." 
# "including A, B, C, and D."

cat_counts = Counter()
for bid, count in review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(bid, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Find the list of categories between keywords and period
    # Remove location prefix
    desc = re.sub(r'Located at [^\.]+\.\s*', '', desc)
    
    # Find categories after specific phrases
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "in X, Y, Z"
    m = re.search(r'\bin\s+([A-Z][^\.]+)(?:\.|\band\b)', desc)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        # Split by comma or 'and'
        parts = re.split(r',|\band\b', text)
        categories = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 2: "including X, Y, Z"
    if not categories:
        m = re.search(r'including\s+([A-Z][^\.]+)(?:\.|\band\b)', desc)
        if m:
            text = m.group(1)
            parts = re.split(r',|\band\b', text)
            categories = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 3: Last items in description (simpler approach)
    if not categories:
        # Remove introductory phrases
        desc_clean = re.sub(r'^[^,]*offers[^,]*,\s*', '', desc)
        desc_clean = re.sub(r'^[^,]*provides[^,]*,\s*', '', desc_clean)
        desc_clean = re.sub(r'^[^,]*specializes[^,]*,\s*', '', desc_clean)
        
        # Split by comma, take first few items (avoiding location info)
        parts = desc_clean.split(',')
        categories = []
        for part in parts[:5]:  # Limit to first 5 to avoid location parts
            part = part.strip()
            # Skip if contains location indicators or is too short
            if any(x in part.lower() for x in ['located', 'rd', 'st.', 'street', 'road', 'at', 'in', 'this']):
                continue
            if part and len(part) > 3:
                categories.append(part)
    
    # Clean categories
    clean_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        # Remove common endings
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+and$', '', cat)
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+to\s+[^,]+$', '', cat)
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+for\s+[^,]+$', '', cat)
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+making\s+[^,]+$', '', cat)
        cat = cat.strip()
        
        if cat and len(cat) > 3 and cat not in ['Active Life']:
            clean_categories.append(cat)
    
    # Limit to most likely categories (max 3)
    for cat in list(set(clean_categories))[:3]:
        cat_counts[cat] += count

# Show results
print(f'Found {len(review_counts)} businesses with {len(cat_counts)} categories')
print('Top 10 categories by total reviews:')
for cat, total in cat_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {total}')

# Get top 5
top_5 = cat_counts.most_common(5)
print('\nFinal Top 5:')
for cat, total in top_5:
    print(f'  {cat}: {total}')

result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews data
reviews_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = reviews_data

# Load businesses data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:68']

# Create mapping
business_id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[bid] += 1

# Extract categories
# Most descriptions follow patterns like:
# "services in A, B, C, and D." 
# "including A, B, C, and D."

cat_counts = Counter()
for bid, count in review_counts.items():
    desc = business_id_to_desc.get(bid, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Find the list of categories between keywords and period
    # Remove location prefix
    desc = re.sub(r'Located at [^\.]+\.\s*', '', desc)
    
    # Find categories after specific phrases
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "in X, Y, Z"
    m = re.search(r'\bin\s+([A-Z][^\.]+)(?:\.|\band\b)', desc)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        # Split by comma or 'and'
        parts = re.split(r',|\band\b', text)
        categories = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 2: "including X, Y, Z"
    if not categories:
        m = re.search(r'including\s+([A-Z][^\.]+)(?:\.|\band\b)', desc)
        if m:
            text = m.group(1)
            parts = re.split(r',|\band\b', text)
            categories = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Pattern 3: Last items in description (simpler approach)
    if not categories:
        # Remove introductory phrases
        desc_clean = re.sub(r'^[^,]*offers[^,]*,\s*', '', desc)
        desc_clean = re.sub(r'^[^,]*provides[^,]*,\s*', '', desc_clean)
        desc_clean = re.sub(r'^[^,]*specializes[^,]*,\s*', '', desc_clean)
        
        # Split by comma, take first few items (avoiding location info)
        parts = desc_clean.split(',')
        categories = []
        for part in parts[:5]:  # Limit to first 5 to avoid location parts
            part = part.strip()
            # Skip if contains location indicators or is too short
            if any(x in part.lower() for x in ['located', 'rd', 'st.', 'street', 'road', 'at', 'in', 'this']):
                continue
            if part and len(part) > 3:
                categories.append(part)
    
    # Clean categories
    clean_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        # Remove common endings
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+and$', '', cat)
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+to\s+[^,]+$', '', cat)
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+for\s+[^,]+$', '', cat)
        cat = re.sub(r'\s+making\s+[^,]+$', '', cat)
        cat = cat.strip()
        
        if cat and len(cat) > 3 and cat not in ['Active Life']:
            clean_categories.append(cat)
    
    # Limit to most likely categories (max 3)
    for cat in list(set(clean_categories))[:3]:
        cat_counts[cat] += count

# Show results
print(f'Found {len(review_counts)} businesses with {len(cat_counts)} categories')
print('Top 10 categories by total reviews:')
for cat, total in cat_counts.most_common(10):
    print(f'  {cat}: {total}')

# Get top 5
top_5 = cat_counts.most_common(5)
print('\nFinal Top 5:')
for cat, total in top_5:
    print(f'  {cat}: {total}')

result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews (file path)
reviews_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses (list)
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:68']

# Map business_id to description
id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[bid] += 1

print('Reviews loaded:', len(reviews), 'Businesses reviewed:', len(review_counts))

# Extract categories - simpler approach
cat_totals = Counter()
details = []

for bid, count in review_counts.items():
    desc = id_to_desc.get(bid, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Remove location prefix
    desc = desc.split('.', 1)[-1] if '.' in desc else desc
    
    # Find categories (usually after keywords like "in", "including", "specializes")
    categories = []
    
    # Split by comma and analyze each part
    parts = desc.split(',')
    for part in parts:
        part = part.strip()
        # Skip parts with location info
        if any(x in part.lower() for x in ['located', 'rd', 'st.', 'road', 'street', 'avenue', 'blvd']):
            continue
        # Skip introductory phrases
        if any(x in part.lower() for x in ['this facility', 'this establishment', 'this business', 'this premier destination']):
            part = part.split('providing', 1)[-1].split('offers', 1)[-1].split('specializes', 1)[-1].strip()
        
        # Clean the part
        part = part.replace('providing a range of services in', '').replace('offering a wide range of services, including', '').strip()
        part = part.replace('offering', '').replace('specializes in', '').replace('and', '').strip()
        
        if part and len(part) > 3:
            categories.append(part)
    
    # Limit categories per business
    for cat in list(set(categories))[:3]:
        cat_totals[cat] += count
        if len(details) < 3:
            details.append({'business': bid, 'reviews': count, 'category': cat})

print('Found', len(cat_totals), 'categories')
print('Top 10:')
for cat, total in cat_totals.most_common(10):
    print('', cat, ':', total)

# Top 5
top_5 = cat_totals.most_common(5)
result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5, 'sample': details})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews (file path)
reviews_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses (list)
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:68']

# Map business_id to description
id_to_desc = {}
for b in businesses:
    id_to_desc[b['business_id']] = b.get('description', '')

# Count reviews per business
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    bid = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[bid] += 1

print('Reviews loaded:', len(reviews), 'Businesses reviewed:', len(review_counts))

# Extract categories - simpler approach
cat_totals = Counter()
details = []

for bid, count in review_counts.items():
    desc = id_to_desc.get(bid, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Remove location prefix
    desc = desc.split('.', 1)[-1] if '.' in desc else desc
    
    # Find categories (usually after keywords like "in", "including", "specializes")
    categories = []
    
    # Split by comma and analyze each part
    parts = desc.split(',')
    for part in parts:
        part = part.strip()
        # Skip parts with location info
        if any(x in part.lower() for x in ['located', 'rd', 'st.', 'road', 'street', 'avenue', 'blvd']):
            continue
        # Skip introductory phrases
        if any(x in part.lower() for x in ['this facility', 'this establishment', 'this business', 'this premier destination']):
            part = part.split('providing', 1)[-1].split('offers', 1)[-1].split('specializes', 1)[-1].strip()
        
        # Clean the part
        part = part.replace('providing a range of services in', '').replace('offering a wide range of services, including', '').strip()
        part = part.replace('offering', '').replace('specializes in', '').replace('and', '').strip()
        
        if part and len(part) > 3:
            categories.append(part)
    
    # Limit categories per business
    for cat in list(set(categories))[:3]:
        cat_totals[cat] += count
        if len(details) < 3:
            details.append({'business': bid, 'reviews': count, 'category': cat})

print('Found', len(cat_totals), 'categories')
print('Top 10:')
for cat, total in cat_totals.most_common(10):
    print('', cat, ':', total)

# Top 5
top_5 = cat_totals.most_common(5)
result = json.dumps({'top_5_categories': top_5, 'sample': details})
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load reviews (file path)
reviews_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load businesses (list from MongoDB)
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:68']

# Create business_id to description map
id_to_desc = {b['business_id']: b.get('description', '') for b in businesses}

# Count reviews per business (convert business_ref to business_id)
review_counts = Counter()
for r in reviews:
    business_id = r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    review_counts[business_id] += 1

# Manual category extraction based on description patterns
category_counts = Counter()
business_categories = {}

# Manually define known categories based on inspection
# Most descriptions list categories after keywords like "services in", "including", etc.

# Process each business
for business_id, review_total in review_counts.items():
    desc = id_to_desc.get(business_id, '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Find categories after "services in" or similar
    categories = []
    
    # Split description into parts
    parts = desc.split(',')
    
    for part in parts:
        part = part.strip()
        
        # Skip location info and generic phrases
        if any(x in part.lower() for x in ['located at', 'offers', 'provides', 'this facility', 'this', 'establishment']):
            continue
        
        # Clean the part
        clean_cat = part.replace('and', '').replace('.', '').strip()
        
        # Collect reasonable category names
        if clean_cat and 3 < len(clean_cat) < 60:
            categories.append(clean_cat)
    
    # Add to counts (limit per business to avoid duplication)
    for cat in list(set(categories))[:3]:  # Max 3 categories per business
        category_counts[cat] += review_total
    
    business_categories[business_id] = categories

# Get top 5 categories
if category_counts:
    top_5 = category_counts.most_common(5)
    result = {'top_5_categories': top_5}
else:
    result = {'top_5_categories': [], 'note': 'No categories found'}

print('Result:', json.dumps(result))
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))
